page: Microsoft Azure D4s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2166.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.34 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics show relative limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 3990.58 and the multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 26.13 GB/sec fall into the lower performance tier. Memory read bandwidth is also constrained, measuring 36.73 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Conversely, the processor's advanced instruction sets, including Intel AMX and AVX-512, support average-tier performance in specialized tasks like LLM inference for small 135M models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D4s_v6 provides a balanced resource density for workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of local NVMe storage. The high single-core clock speed of 4.2 GHz makes it suitable for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, and database operations that do not demand high multi-threaded throughput or massive memory bandwidth. It is appropriate for general-purpose workloads, basic application hosting, and small-scale text or image processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv6 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It provides 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores with hyper-threading) and 16.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPUs. The instance exhibits strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2166.0 and fast single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak, as evidenced by lower stress-ng multi-core and uncached read bandwidth scores. Equipped with Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, it handles basic vector and matrix operations efficiently. This server is best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, and database operations that benefit from dedicated compute resources and high single-core speeds rather than heavy multi-threaded parallelization.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering strong single-core performance alongside moderate multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual machine offering strong single-core clock speeds and balanced memory allocation for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong single-core performance in compression and text processing benchmarks
No local storage or GPU, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Supports advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, and database operations
